text: What if I told you most of your energy comes from a hidden process? This is oxidative phosphorylation, the final step of cellular respiration where energy is produced. It happens in the mitochondria, often called the powerhouse of the cell. But how? Electrons move through proteins, creating a proton gradient. This gradient powers ATP synthase, making ATP. ATP fuels everything from muscle contractions to brain functions. It's life energy in action. Want to dive deeper into how your body works? Hit subscribe for more!
